reason: Adjusting the retry limit in /etc/security/pwquality.conf is a standard password policy hardening step that only affects the number of prompts allowed during password changes; it does not interfere with existing authentication mechanisms or system boot processes.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Setting retry too low could cause legitimate users to be locked out after mistyped passwords; Incorrect syntax or conflicting settings in other pwquality.conf.d files could cause PAM to reject password changes
